description_long: This is a classic tiered stone fountain with three circular basins stacked vertically, each decreasing in diameter as they ascend. The lowest and largest basin forms a wide, shallow pool with a thick, rounded rim, while the two upper basins are supported by cylindrical columns and feature gently sloping sides. At the top, a small decorative finial sits at the center, crowning the structure. The surfaces display a weathered, off-white stone texture with subtle stains and discoloration, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. The overall design is symmetrical and elegant, typical of public plazas, parks, or courtyards. There are no visible water jets or flowing water, and the basins appear dry.
description: A three-tiered, weathered stone fountain with circular basins and a decorative finial, featuring a classic symmetrical design and aged surface.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full tiered structure, with all three basins and the central finial clearly visible.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view revealing the depth of the basins and the thickness of the outer rim, with subtle shadowing on the right.
description_view_2: Another angled view similar to view 1, highlighting the symmetry and the layered construction of the fountain.
description_view_3: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, emphasizing the circular form and the even distribution of the basins.
attribute_ornamentation_details: The fountain features a simple, classical design with a smooth finial at the top and rounded, unadorned rims. There are no visible engravings, motifs, or elaborate decorative elements.
attribute_basin_structure: The fountain has three circular, tiered basins, each with a gently sloped profile and a thick, rounded rim. The largest basin forms the base, with two progressively smaller basins stacked above, all supported by cylindrical columns.
attribute_jet_or_spray_pattern: No visible water jets or spray patterns are present; the basins appear dry and inactive.
